steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the midsegment
steps.content: M is the midpoint of PQ and N is the midpoint of QR, so MN is the midsegment of triangle PQR connecting midpoints of two sides. By the Triangle Midsegment Theorem, MN is parallel to PR and $MN = \frac{1}{2} PR$.

steps.title: Solve for PR and test all choices
steps.content:
$$PR = 2 \times MN = 2 \times 15 = 30$$
  • Choice A — 30: $2 \times 15 = 30$. ✓
  • Choice B — 28: Does not equal $2 \times 15$. ✗
  • Choice C — 24: Equals $QM + MN = 9 + 15$, but adding those segments does not give PR. ✗
  • Choice D — 18: Equals $2 \times QM = 2 \times 9$, but QM is not the midsegment. ✗
A is correct.
